reviews.review_description:
After 30 hours in beta, I can confidently say SWARM nails what it sets out to do. This is perpetual motion arcade shooting at its finest. You literally never stop moving, grappling from platform to platform while unloading on waves of robots. 

The physics feel weighted and responsive, with hand-over-hand climbing and pull-down techniques that make traversal genuinely joyful. I expected motion sickness but got none, even with my middling VR legs. Five zones with environmental hazards keep things fresh, boss fights feel appropriately epic, and the cel-shaded visuals are crisp even when chaos erupts. 

The difficulty settings are thoughtfully done, letting you zone out on casual or get instantly shredded on hard. That said, this is pure arcade action. If score chasing isn't your thing, you'll bounce off quickly. 

For the full review, search YouTube for 6DOF Reviews SWARM.
This game is really cool and fun to play, I like the idea to have different gun drops and the lightning platforms are great. It’s not to hard and really good I recommend it

As a 55-year-old woman, I absolutely love it, and so do my nephews, aged 8 and 11. We gather around the big TV, taking turns to play. Not only is it a lot of fun, but it also gets us moving and gives our brains a good workout with quick actions and sharp reflexes.
25 MINUTES IN THIS GAME AND I LOOOOOVVVVEEE IT. ITS ABSOLUTELY WELL MADE(wish you could shoot while grappling)

like that guy overthemeta or whatever leaving a 1 star review for this its so annoying seeing like 50 year olds playing this and saying “oh the swinging is bad” *insert dumb accent* yeah it might be for YOU the swinging is fine btw just because you dont you your joysticks to move and its hard to play while sitting in your hospus house doesnt mean that the teen rating is bad. ITS RATED TEEN FOR A REASON some 50 year olds isng gonna wanna play this. And “oh the tutorial is forced” *insert dumb accent* like it might be but just deal with it the tutorial is like 10 minutes and it may be forced somewhat but isnt that what most tutorials are? with the “press x button to reload” or “press A to jump” while in this tiny corridor or smth like younplay the tutorial in one of the maps. The gameplay is challenging but not too difficult and the swinging is like spider man with guns; you pull yourself to go faster which is a original concept btw (as far as ive seen) and its just fun when listening to music and swinging and killing stuff but the game hasn’t been updated in like 3 years and probably wont be again so just deal with it. Its a good game with its flaws but is still fun enough to play and have fun. Also going to that “overthemeta” guy again saying you can only play in 30 minute increments yeah FOR YOU the audience of this game is teenagers and young adults not old geezers who can only play for 30 minutes before there nurse has them take a nap. Like old people have some nerve trashing on a completely fine game for no reason.

You have two grappling hooks.
You have two guns.
You own.
A lot.

Kinda reminded me of Ricochet a little, which was released by Valve in 2000. 

Although I just started my VR experience, I have tried many games and modded non-vr games. This one definitely stands out.
Perfect show case for the quest. I could not imagine playing this wired. I had a lot of fun.

So you think you can get away with pricing a $5 dollar game at $25 dollars? Well heres a 1 star review to balance it for you. It took me 3 minutes of playing to realize this is the worst grappling game Ive ever played. The physics are faked and the feeling is unnatural. Your tutorial is forced and boring. When Most people can only play VR in 30 Minute increments you need to pull me in quick. Never got to use a gun and dont care to because you seemed unbothered about forcing me through a tutorial I didnt want or need.. Now, if this was a FIVE DOLLAR or less game I would have left a much more positive review. Cant believe enough people fell for this for you to make a sequel game. PASS ON THIS GAME UNLESS ITS FREE FRIENDS.
Just such a good game where your going to save the world with your grapiling hooks and guns to save the world really recommend this game

I’ve only played, like, 20 minutes of the game, and already it’s great. I love the cell-shaded art style, as it’s one of my favorites, and the gameplay is really fun. It’s pretty much like you’re Spider-Man, except you just kill everything. 

The only gripe I have with the game is that the dialogue is unrealistic. I mean, this game isn’t SUPPOSED to be be realistic, it’s just that the main character is STUPIDLY overconfident. 

“You finished the training already?”
“The course wasn’t training me. I was training your tech to keep up with me.”

“They’re attacking the university!”
“Great. If I wanted to learn, I wouldn’t have quit 3rd grade.”

Stuff like that. If I can recognize this dialogue goofiness at 14 years old, then something’s gotta change. However, the more I think about it, the more I realize that the main character being this way is on purpose, because that’s kinda how the old comic book characters act. Oh well. It’s overall a great game and a must-have
great graphics amazing game play

reviews.review_title: It’s a blast, but it’s NOT multiplayer
reviews.review_description:
This game is not multiplayer (in a traditional sense) at all. I wanted to play a good multiplayer game with a family member, we got this game and kept thinking “maybe we have to get past the into levels first”… nope. 

I think the “multiplayer” is seeing a score board? 

The game is a ton of fun if you’re looking for a single player game though.

reviews.review_title: if spider man and doom had a kid
reviews.review_description: basically the best game i ever played so far on vr! the physics is good, the guns are good, that's it the grappling hook is good it's really good it's super good basically having the time of life! the only downpart is no multiplayer i don't know why it doesn't have any but still good game.
What more is there to say

False advertising. Multiplayer is NOT a synchronised score board. Saw multiplayer and thought this will be great with my kids. Nope.. nothing but dissapointment and glorified space invaders
i Love this game and the joke about "spiderman with guns" Isn't actually a joke.
Great game.  Best single player arcade game.

If this is one of your first VR experiences, then you are in for a treat. If you are a seasoned VR gamer and have been playing titles for years, then this is just another arena shooter with solid mechanics.

If you enjoy this, I'd recommend it. It takes these mechanics and adds a story campaign and an open world.

Swarm is definitely worth a sale purchase or a try if you have a horizon plus subscription.
this game is so good that i think everyone should play it
all i gotta say is it's amazing
SUPER ADDICTING but it said multiplayer and yet when I got it it wasn’t, still SUPER fun (still recommend buying or getting with quest plus

reviews.review_title: Definitely one of the best vr games ever
reviews.review_description: I truly thank the developers for letting us experience something like this for free, this is the best swinging game in this platform! I will buy and experience Swarm 2 as soon as i can

I haven’t played video games since the 80s, pre-Nintendo and this game looked cartoonish so I had no intention of even trying it out. Luckily I opened it by mistake and decided to give it a shot. It is easily the craziest, coolest and most exhilarating arcade game I’ve ever played. It’s indescribable!  Amazing!  And impressive.

reviews.review_description: I play VR every single day this is only game that gives me vertigo and nausea, movement is crazy, cannot enjoy.

this game is actually really fun they manage to not give you motion sickness at all you can swing all over the place and shoot at these flying drones get points and the music is aggressive but fun. highly recommended to anyone who likes to play shooters and is looking to get a full experience out of their vr headset. not serious enough that feels like you have to play it all day every day but also fun enough that feels like when you're playing it you're actually having a blast

I’ve had this game for a little while now but my backlog is crazy. I finally decided to give Swarm a try and I wasn’t sure what to expect. I already play Resist which I loved but I was kind of expecting more of the same with Swarm. Though it is similar to Resist in some ways,it is a completely different experience and equally amazing. It is so much fun zipping around and blasting robots out of the sky. I highly recommend this game and now I’m definitely going to have to add Swarm 2 to my library

developer_response.body: Hey Smenjsjsart1228, thanks for you feedback, sorry you didn't love playing Swarm! If you'd like to avoid having to turn your body around to face enemies, you can go into settings and enable snap turn or smooth turn, and then use the control stick on to turn!

reviews.review_description: graphics is very weak And  Boring game

developer_response.body: Hey Unitedonweb, thanks for the feedback, sorry Swarm didn't meet your expectations. The cell-shaded graphics in Swarm are made specifically to reduce motion sickness. The best weapons and abilities are unlocked later in the campaign, and really change up the gameplay - if you want some tips on how to unlock them, visit us at discord.gg/greensky or check out this helpful video! https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hRjMU1AYgqw&ab_channel=GreenskyGames

reviews.review_description: The tutorial doesn’t explain the game, and grappling is hard to follow or understand in the tutorial. I totally don’t understand how or why the water matters and there aren’t any helpful guides on the app. I’m new to VR and found myself frustrated to the point of putting down the controller. It would help if the concept were explained and there was more detailed guidance on the game. So disappointed

developer_response.body:
Hey quills.2023

Thank you for your feedback, sorry Swarm's tutorial didn't help you in the way it should have.

If you want to learn more about the basics of the game, we have a tips and tricks video here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hRjMU1AYgqw&ab_channel=GreenskyGames

And if you want to learn more from the best players in the world, head over to discord.gg/greensky. The community there is super friendly, knowledgeable, and always willing to share their skills.

Otherwise, you can always email us at support@greenskygamesstudio.com for anything else!

reviews.review_description:
I thought this game was going to be a lot better than it is because of the top-ratings and 5start reviews… but it feels very lacking and falls into the “phone-app” category for me where “i will play it for a week and then never touch it again”. 

Definitely not a 5 star “top-selling” game, but easy to pick up and put down, if you are looking for that.
This is a lot of fun. New to VR, enjoy this because you’re not sitting around, I feel like I’m actually in the movements. Neato torpedo!

reviews.review_description:
Update:
Was kinda hoping for some new content… maybe a map or three? I love this game but it’s replayability starts to decline after a few months with absolutely nothing new added.. Are there any plans to ever do anything with some multiplayer or is it just gonna be in beta  forever?  Kinda feels like it’s been abandoned… say it isn’t so!

Taking the lead of some other players and reducing my rating  from 5 to one as we await the promised content-

If somebody pays attention maybe I’ll put it back up to five once they actually do what they said they were gonna do.￼




This is just nuts! If you were playing seated make sure that you’re in a chair that spins in a circle! This is very fast moving and super fun! Also sports the Humor of a G rated Duke Nukem!  The first review I read said “Spider Man With Guns”, and I was in, Baby! Schweeeeeet!

You’re gonna see a few reviews where people say “meh”- Trust me when I tell you that those people just gave up before they got  good at it-  There’s a false ceiling when you start off before you start yo intuitively react… 

Once you really understand the mechanics of it there’s really nothing like it. It’s incredibly exhilarating and always challenging!

reviews.review_description: I played a beta of this a couple of weeks ago and it is without a doubt one of if not THE MOST FUN GAME I HAVE PLAYED ON QUEST! I have been dying for this day to come and it finally has.  Never bought a game 1 minute after it went on sale but I was literally counting the minutes down. Spiderman meets Galaga.  Flying around using grappling hooks as fast as you can to avoid the aliens and their fire while at the same time constantly firing at them to destroy them.  Insane excitement and action. I can not recommend this game enough!!!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the support! Check Swarm 2 out here: https://www.meta.com/experiences/5791805387504648/?utmsource=r

reviews.review_description: I bought this game thinking I was going to be swinging from buildings and fighting creatures with other players at the same time. But instead I was pretty much in the air swinging from objects shooting by myself, it feels lonely and eventually boring after a while 😩.

developer_response.body: Hi Yos-sy, stay tuned, I think you're going to quite like some updates we have coming.

reviews.review_description: There’s something beautiful about flying around destroying robots like you’re in Attack on Titan with the gun option. Just beautiful

developer_response.body: Thanks for cheering us on! Swarm 2 is now live! Here's the link: https://www.meta.com/experiences/5791805387504648/?utmsource=r

reviews.review_description: I was hesitant to play this game because I got my quest a few months ago but still don’t feel that my “VR legs” are fully grown yet. I’m better than I was, especially with stick locomotion, so again I was hesitant. I played for the first time and was ASTOUNDED that I didn’t feel sick AT ALL. I don’t know if it’s me or the game but I can’t imagine it’s me so, good job devs.👏🏻👏🏻

developer_response.body: Thanks for the awesome review! Swarm 2 is now available:  https://www.meta.com/experiences/5791805387504648/?utmsource=r

reviews.review_description: It reminds me of the old 80’s game Galaga, but with a new twist. Just like everyone else has already said, it is like Spider-Man but with guns in the setting of Galaga. I did not experience any motion sickness even with all the movements involved in the game. I would recommend it.
